what_it_does: Defines a design-only desktop form spec for translating existing phone gallery screens into fixed 1440x900 desktop mockups using the same component classes and design tokens.
observations: Frames desktop as a fixed, non-scrolling 1440x900 canvas with a wide-aspect relayout of phone screens.
Proposes a `.desk` root in `ui/phone.css` parallel to `.screen`, with a 236px sidebar lane and content lane.
Treats `.p-rail` as the desktop left sidebar rather than introducing a new navigation component.
Separates shared `.desk` skeleton CSS from per-screen mockup layout CSS such as `.cols`, widened `.grid`, and `.md`.
Defines three initial desktop mockups: `desk-home`, `desk-dashboard`, and `desk-ledger`.
Grounds `desk-ledger` master/detail as a spatial version of the mobile drill-in relationship.
Lists exact downstream files DNA would touch and explicitly marks several shared files out of scope.
Includes a by-sight verification checklist focused on no scrollbar, sidebar reading, one gold action, dashboard dark ground, detail-pane reuse, thumbnails, and shared CSS footprint.
standouts: The file makes the height budget the controlling design constraint: desktop gains width but little height.
The proposed desktop layer is intentionally limited to relayouts of existing `.p-*` classes.
Master/detail is explicitly justified as spatialized drill-in rather than a newly invented screen type.
conventions: Uses evidence labels such as Observed, Verified, and Proposed to distinguish source readings from design decisions.
Uses `DNA adds...` phrasing for changes owned outside this design document.
Keeps canvas dimensions and absolute frame geometry as raw px while reserving tokens for spacing, type, radius, and color.
Places per-screen arrangement CSS inside mockup files, matching the convention described in the document.
Avoids introducing new shared component classes in the proposed shared CSS footprint.
concerns: The document is dense and relies heavily on cited file paths and line references that are not reproduced here.
Several proposed CSS snippets are illustrative but not complete implementation text.
The spec depends on visual judgment criteria such as whether the sidebar reads correctly, which cannot be resolved inside the file alone.
notes: A design handoff spec for building three fixed-size desktop mockups from the existing phone component vocabulary.
questions: What exact labels or brand treatment should appear in the optional desktop sidebar if icons alone read as stretched mobile navigation?
Which ledger row is selected by default in `desk-ledger`?
Should the desktop manifest group name be treated as fixed wording or as a suggested label?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the actual content: it resolves a pending desktop-screens design ghost into a buildable, visually verifiable spec while leaving implementation to DNA.
intention_signals: It seems intended to constrain a future implementation so desktop mockups feel like the same application face at a wider tier while limiting shared CSS and avoiding new component vocabulary.
novelty: Spatializing mobile drill-in as desktop master/detail while keeping the detail pane grounded in existing screen-local components.
contribution: Contributes a neutral desktop interpretation layer: fixed 1440x900 canvas rules, `.desk` relayout guidance, sidebar treatment for `.p-rail`, three proposed desktop mockups, and verification criteria a fused system could retain as design intent.
summary_for_embedding: Design-only desktop form spec proposing fixed 1440x900 gallery mockups that reuse existing phone `.p-*` components through a `.desk` root, per-screen grids, sidebar navigation, and three proof screens.
intention_for_embedding: This file was written to guide DNA in building and visually verifying desktop versions of application screens without inventing new shared components or changing out-of-scope gallery infrastructure.
